draft.introduction: The messianic throne oracle, declaring a child born to rule with divine titles. The fourfold name—Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace—became central to christological debate.

translation.text: For to us a child is born, to us a son is given; and the government shall be upon his shoulder, and his name shall be called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace. Of the increase of his government and of peace there shall be no end.
translation.original_text: ὅτι παιδίον ἐγεννήθη ἡμῖν υἱὸς καὶ ἐδόθη ἡμῖν

translator_notes.note: The LXX renders the divine titles differently from the Hebrew, calling the child μεγάλης βουλῆς ἄγγελος (“messenger of great counsel”). The Hebrew “Mighty God” (El Gibbor) is more explicitly divine, raising the question of whether the Messiah was understood as divine in pre-Christian Judaism.
